summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the definition of 'Bias'?

"Bias" means: A systematic error in data collection or analysis that consistently skews results in one direction; not reducible by increasing sample size. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Selection bias (undercoverage)'?

"Selection bias (undercoverage)" means: Systematic exclusion of certain population members from the sampling frame, producing a sample that doesn't represent the population. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Response bias'?

"Response bias" means: Systematic error caused by the wording of questions, interviewer influence, or respondent dishonesty leading to inaccurate answers. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Nonresponse bias'?

"Nonresponse bias" means: Occurs when individuals selected for a sample cannot be contacted or refuse to participate, and non-respondents differ systematically from respondents. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Confounding'?

"Confounding" means: When the effects of two variables on the response cannot be distinguished from each other because they are intertwined. The other options describe different concepts.

summary_sections.content_html:
Real-World Applications
